template.name: Incident Response Task
template.description: AM checklist for flagship website downtime or performance incidents. Designed to prevent important steps from being skipped under pressure, not as a sequential workflow.

tasks.name: Confirm you are the AM or BAM taking this incident
tasks.notes: If you are the BAM stepping in because the AM is unavailable, note that in the Slack thread.

tasks.name: Open a thread in #web-developers-only — state you are on the problem
tasks.notes: This is the incident's running log. All updates, findings, and decisions go here.

tasks.name: Senior Director(s) notified
tasks.notes: Business hours: Slack @here or @channel, 5 minute wait. If no response after 5 minutes, text. Off-hours or non-work day: start the #web-developers-only thread and text Senior Directors directly.

tasks.name: Senior Director posts to #urban-general
tasks.notes: Message should say something like: "We are aware that urban.org is experiencing downtime/performance issues. We will provide an update when this is resolved." This is their responsibility, not yours — but confirm it happens.

tasks.name: COMM team member assigned to keep incident timeline and notes
tasks.notes: This is easy to assume someone else is handling. Confirm it explicitly.

tasks.name: Check Pantheon status
tasks.notes: Is the issue on Pantheon's end? Check their status page and any platform-level alerts before assuming it's a code or traffic problem.

tasks.name: Check New Relic — identify onset and throughput pattern
tasks.notes: Look for steep increases in traffic, error rate spikes, or response time degradation. When did it start? Is it getting worse?

tasks.name: Check Cloudflare or nginx logs for problem IPs or unusual traffic patterns
tasks.notes: Look for blocks of IPs hitting /search paths or other repeated patterns. Note any ASNs associated with the problem IPs.

tasks.name: Identify incident type and apply mitigation steps
tasks.notes:
Known types:

1. IP traversal of /search paths:
   - Cloudflare: Turn on Attack Mode
   - Drupal: Disable Search view, Facets, and Search block from Block Layout
   - Cloudflare: Identify and block the ASN associated with problem IPs
   - Drupal: Once stable, review and tighten rate limiting
   - Schedule ASN block removal after enough time has passed

2. Solr search outage (Pantheon Solr service failure):
   - Confirm this is search-only, not a broader outage
   - Drupal: Verify Solr server is active and connected
   - Check that all search indexes are fully indexed
   - Confirm Solr schema is current and meets minimum version
   - If schema is outdated, update and re-index via drush (not Drupal UI — it will time out)
   - If re-indexing fails: contact Pantheon Support immediately, notify Senior Director(s) and Associate Director of Web Development & Product Management, post in #web-developers-only

3. Other: document findings in the Slack thread and escalate as needed.

tasks.name: Escalation check — does this need Pantheon Support?
tasks.notes: Escalate to Pantheon if: re-indexing fails, the issue appears to be a platform-level Solr service failure, or you cannot identify or resolve the cause after initial mitigation steps.

tasks.name: Post progress updates in #web-developers-only thread
tasks.notes: Keep the thread current as findings and actions develop. Senior Directors need visibility to field questions from the rest of the organization.

tasks.name: Confirm site is stable before declaring resolution
tasks.notes: Check New Relic — Web Transactions Time graph should be settled. Don't declare resolution while metrics are still elevated.

tasks.name: Post resolution update in #web-developers-only thread
tasks.notes: Include: what the issue was, what was done to resolve it, and any ongoing monitoring or cleanup steps in progress.

tasks.name: Senior Director posts all-clear to #urban-general
tasks.notes: Confirm this happens — same as the initial notification, this is their message to send, not yours.

tasks.name: Any pending cleanup steps scheduled
tasks.notes: Examples: remove ASN blocks after enough time has passed, re-enable any Drupal features that were disabled during mitigation (Search view, Facets, Search block), review rate limiting strategy.

tasks.name: Jira ticket created and linked in Slack thread
tasks.notes: Document the incident: onset time, alert source, what was found, what was done, time to resolution. This is the record for future reference and pattern tracking.

tasks.name: Brief retrospective note added to Jira — what would have made this faster or easier?
tasks.notes: Even one sentence is useful. This is how the playbook improves over time.
